title: Conflict Diagnosis and Semantic Resolution · History Brief: Semantic Resolution
summary: Resolve an integration conflict by reconstructing both intents and verifying behavior rather than deleting markers until Git accepts the file. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git semantic resolution increment in a conflict reproduction, resolution, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Construct a new result from requirements, tests, and both histories rather than treating resolution as text selection.
Read conflict markers and diff views while obtaining surrounding base and commit intent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Semantic Resolution
steps.instruction:
theory case — Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=5, and repair limit=6 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Semantic Resolution contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for construct a new result from requirements, tests, and both histories rather than treating resolution as text selection.

content.text: Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=5, and repair limit=6 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Semantic Resolution condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: gitrevisions Manual — Controls revision names, ancestry, range, exclusion, reflog, and object selection semantics.

steps.title: Bound Semantic Resolution
steps.instruction:
theory case — Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=6, and repair limit=7 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Semantic Resolution application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for construct a new result from requirements, tests, and both histories rather than treating resolution as text selection.

content.text: Construct a new result from requirements, tests, and both histories rather than treating resolution as text selection. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=5, and repair limit=6 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue. A defensible solution applies Semantic Resolution, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a conflict reproduction, resolution, and regression record.

content.code:
base=$(git merge-base main repair)
printf 'base=%s\n' "$base"
git diff "$base"..repair
git ls-files --unmerged
# Resolve intent, stage, test, then continue.
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A syntactically marker-free file is a resolved conflict. This fails the Semantic Resolution contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Semantic Resolution, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Build Semantic Resolution evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-semantic-resolution-1e00ti" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Semantic Resolution into observable performance evidence.

# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]

steps.title: Defend the Semantic Resolution model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Semantic Resolution: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=6, and repair limit=7 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Construct a new result from requirements, tests, and both histories rather than treating resolution as text selection.
